title: Trusted Postgres Architect 23.44.0 release notes
navTitle: Version 23.44.0

---

Released: 10 June 2026


New features, enhancements, bug fixes, and other changes in Trusted Postgres Architect 23.44.0 include the following:

## Highlights

- Support for upgrading components in a PGD-X cluster
- Improved robustness of PGD upgrades
- The EFM user is no longer a superuser by default

## Enhancements

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now supports upgrade of components in the PGD-X architecture.</summary><hr/><p>In the upgrade playbook for PGD-X, additional steps that ensure the upgrade of selected components
(PgBouncer, pg-backup-api, Barman, PEM server) via tpaexec (<code>tpaexec upgrade (...) --components=&lt;component&gt;</code>)
have been included, so individual components can be upgraded without the need to upgrade the entire cluster.
This upgrade process is included as a standalone feature for component upgrades, as well as minor PGD-X upgrades
and major upgrades to the PGD-X architecture.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA can now deploy the EFM database user with only the minimum required permissions.</summary><hr/><p>On a regular EFM deployment, TPA granted the EFM user SUPERUSER
privileges.
A new variable called 'efm_user_is_superuser' has been introduced, where
TPA deploys the EFM user as a Postgres SUPERUSER, or as a regular login user
that is a member of 'efm_role', a role created by TPA with only the specific
catalog functions and predefined roles (pg_monitor, pg_read_all_settings,
pg_read_all_stats) that EFM requires (the variable is set to 'false' by default
when creating new clusters via <code>tpaexec configure</code>, removing this variable from
the configuration file causes 'efm_user_is_superuser' to be treated as true).</p>
<p>The variable acts as a switch and can be toggled with a regular deployment, and
can be used on already deployed clusters to switch the privileges of the EFM user.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now supports Ansible Automation Platform 2.6.</summary><hr/><p>TPA is now tested with AAP 2.6. Existing execution environments are
compatible with both AAP 2.4 and AAP 2.6.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added support for proxy monitoring during PGD 5 to PGD 6 upgrades.</summary><hr/><p>The <code>enable_proxy_monitoring=yes</code> option to <code>tpaexec upgrade</code> can
now be used during upgrades from PGD-Always-ON (PGD 5) to PGD-X
(PGD 6). Previously it would cause the upgrade to fail immediately
because the monitor attempted to connect to the Connection Manager
port on witness nodes, which do not run Connection Manager. Witness
nodes are now excluded from the list of endpoints the monitor
targets.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now supports non-default hugepage sizes.</summary><hr/><p>TPA's hugepages settings previously assumed the architecture's default
page size (2MB on x86_64), so a configuration that wanted Postgres to
use 1GB hugepages required the user to set the kernel command line,
the <code>vm.nr_hugepages</code> sysctl, and the <code>huge_page_size</code> GUC by hand,
and the values TPA generated worked against them. TPA now supports a
<code>huge_page_size</code> variable (a Postgres-style memory string such as
<code>1GB</code>). When set, TPA reserves pages of that size on the kernel
command line with <code>hugepagesz=</code>, omits <code>vm.nr_hugepages</code> from
<code>/etc/sysctl.conf</code> (which only ever applies to the architecture's
default-size pool), and sets <code>huge_page_size</code> in <code>postgresql.conf</code>
so Postgres draws from the chosen pool. The page count can be
overridden with a new <code>nr_hugepages</code> variable; for backwards
compatibility, an existing <code>sysctl_values['vm.nr_hugepages']</code> is
still honoured when <code>nr_hugepages</code> is not set explicitly. Because
every reservation is taken out of normal memory at boot,
<code>huge_page_size</code> is usually best set on individual Postgres instances
rather than cluster-wide.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added an environment variable option to the repmgr service unit file.</summary><hr/><p>A new variable called 'repmgr_service_environment' has been added to the repmgr service unit template file for repmgr.
This variable allows users to specify custom environment variables that will be set for the repmgr service.</p>
<p>The 'repmgr_service_environment' variable can be defined in the cluster configuration file, as a dictionary where the
keys are the names of the environment variables and the values are the corresponding values for those variables.</p>
<p>This feature allows users to easily set environment variables that may be required for a specific use case.</p>
</details></td><td>56200</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added supported cluster_vars overrides for TPA's choice of Python interpreter and packages.</summary><hr/><p>Two new (or newly-effective) cluster_vars now control how TPA uses
Python on cluster nodes:</p>
<ol>
<li>
<p><code>script_python_interpreter</code> — templates the shebang line of TPA-
installed Python scripts (for example <code>/etc/tpa/postgres-monitor</code>).
Defaults to <code>/usr/bin/env &#123;&#123; python }}</code>, preserving the previous
PATH-based behaviour. Users can set it in <code>cluster_vars</code> to an
absolute interpreter path to pin scripts deterministically — useful
on hosts that have more than one Python installed and where the
default <code>env</code> lookup would find the wrong one.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><code>python_pkg_prefix</code> — controls the name prefix TPA uses when
constructing OS package names (for example <code>python3-psycopg2</code>
versus <code>python311-psycopg2</code>). It was already an internal fact, but
setting it in <code>cluster_vars</code> previously had no effect because
<code>minimal_setup</code>'s output overwrote it. It is now passed through to
<code>minimal_setup</code> as a module parameter, so a user-supplied value
is honoured.</p>
</li>
</ol>
<p>See <code>docs/src/python.md</code> for details, including the recommendation
to couple the two variables on any host that has more than one
Python interpreter installed.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now supports enable_proxy_monitoring during PGD 6 minor upgrades.</summary><hr/><p>The <code>enable_proxy_monitoring=yes</code> option to <code>tpaexec upgrade</code> now
works for PGD 6 to PGD 6 minor upgrades, in addition to the major
upgrade paths where it was already supported. When enabled, TPA
records Connection Manager downtime on each data node during the
rolling fence/restart cycle.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Improved restart and service excluded_tasks coverage.</summary><hr/><p>TPA now respects the <code>restart</code> and <code>service</code> values of <code>excluded_tasks</code>
across all roles that perform service restarts or systemd service
management, including Barman, beacon-agent, EFM, etcd, harp, Patroni,
PEM (agent and server), pgbackupapi, PgBouncer, PGD Proxy, Postgres,
repmgr (restart, switchover, replica final), rsyslog, and OpenVPN.</p>
<p>Previously, users excluding <code>restart</code> or <code>service</code> tasks could still
see unintended restarts in these areas during a deploy. With this
change, all restart and direct service operations are guarded
consistently, so excluded_tasks reliably suppresses them.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>tpaexec reconfigure now accepts --bdr-package-version.</summary><hr/><p>tpaexec reconfigure now accepts --bdr-package-version on BDR-Always-ON,
PGD-Always-ON, PGD-X, and PGD-S clusters, recording the value in
config.yml and applying any version-gated configuration options the
chosen version requires. Currently the only such option is
read_listen_port in default_pgd_proxy_options for PGD 5.5+, matching
what tpaexec configure has been doing since TPA-1454. The new logic
also runs as a require() of the BDR-Always-ON → PGD-Always-ON and
PGD-Always-ON → PGD-X architecture changes, so those upgrade paths
pick up the version-gated options when no --bdr-package-version is
supplied.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now supports enable_proxy_monitoring for PGD 5 minor upgrades.</summary><hr/><p>The enable_proxy_monitoring option now works during PGD 5 minor version
upgrades in PGD-Always-ON clusters. Previously, proxy monitoring was only
supported during BDR 4 to PGD 5 major upgrades. When enabled, the proxy
monitor tracks connection availability through pgd-proxy endpoints
throughout the upgrade process and reports any interruptions.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
</tbody></table>


## Changes

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>Replaced upgrade_legacy.yml with a dedicated upgrade_major_4to5.yml playbook.</summary><hr/><p>The BDR 4→5 major upgrade logic has been extracted from the monolithic
upgrade_legacy.yml into a dedicated playbook. This simplifies the upgrade
path by removing all conditional branching, hardcoding harp as the
failover manager and upgrade_from as version 4. The now-orphaned
upgrade_legacy.yml files have been removed from PGD-Always-ON and
Lightweight architectures.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added a dedicated upgrade_minor_5.yml playbook for BDR 5.x minor upgrades.</summary><hr/><p>This new playbook handles all BDR 5.x minor upgrades. This uses the relevant
logic previously embedded in the monolithic upgrade_legacy.yml, making the upgrade
process easier to maintain.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA no longer passes empty --team and --owner to pemworker.</summary><hr/><p>When registering a Postgres server with PEM, TPA previously passed
<code>--team &quot;&quot;</code> and <code>--owner &quot;&quot;</code> to <code>pemworker --register-server</code> if the
<code>monitoring_team</code> or <code>monitoring_server_owner</code> variables were not
set. Earlier PEM versions treated these empty strings as equivalent
to omitting the option, but PEM 10.5 tightens CLI validation and
rejects empty strings, causing the registration task to fail. TPA
now omits these flags entirely when the corresponding variables are
unset, so server registration succeeds against PEM 10.5 without
requiring any configuration change.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Documented how to run custom playbooks on AAP.</summary><hr/><p>The AAP user documentation now explains how to add custom playbooks
to a cluster and run them through AAP, using the <code>commands/</code>
subdirectory convention and AAP's Duplicate template action.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added missing flags to the tpaexec help output.</summary><hr/><p>'tpaexec help' was missing some flags that are actually supported.
This change adds those flags to the help output.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now rejects pgaudit on EPAS clusters at the start of deploy.</summary><hr/><p>Including <code>pgaudit</code> in <code>extra_postgres_extensions</code> on an EPAS
cluster used to result in a deployment that failed late at
Postgres startup, because pgaudit is not loadable on EPAS.
TPA now detects this combination at the start of deploy and
fails with a message recommending EPAS's built-in audit
features.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added &#123;stop,start,list}-containers to the tpaexec --help output.</summary><hr/><p>The already implemented options for managing docker containers in tpaexec
were featured in the documentation but not in the output of <code>tpaexec --help</code>.
This change ensures that the <code>tpaexec --help</code> command contains a brief summary
of what these commands do.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
</tbody></table>


## Bug Fixes

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue whereby tpaexec reconfigure would not add postgres_distributed during BDR 4 to BDR 5 upgrades.</summary><hr/><p>The 'tpaexec reconfigure' command, when upgrading from BDR 4 to BDR 5, didn't add the
'postgres_distributed' repository, which would result in a failure when trying to download
the new BDR 5 packages.
A fix has been introduced to ensure that the mentioned repository would be included in the
configuration file.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue whereby standby nodes could be promoted to BDR primary candidates.</summary><hr/><p>An issue was found where standby nodes could be selected as BDR primary candidates during deployment,
which could lead to unintended consequences in some cluster scenarios (e.g. when
joining BDR node groups via standby nodes).
This fix ensures that standby nodes are excluded from the list of potential BDR
primary candidates.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue whereby SLES 15 deployments failed when featuring patroni with EDBPGE and EPAS.</summary><hr/><p>When selecting patroni as the failover manager with the EDBPGE/EPAS postgresql flavour, the modules
needed to install etcd-related packages were missing, and some additional adjustments were required to make the deployment work correctly.
This fix ensures that the required packages are installed when deploying on SLES 15 with patroni
as the failover manager, alongside some minor adjustments.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue where requesting PEM agent &lt;9.6 would cause deployment to fail.</summary><hr/><p>TPA uses the <code>pemworker --enable-probe</code> option to enable EFM probes
where required. However this option does not exist before PEM 9.6,
so trying to call it causes an error. This fix adds a version check
to this task meaning it will be skipped when the agent version is
&lt;9.6.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue whereby pgd-cli tasks would run when pgd-cli was not upgraded.</summary><hr/><p>During a postgres-only upgrade (the default), the tasks that log pgd-cli
diagnostic output and wait for write leader elections were always executed
on the first BDR primary, even when pgd-cli had not been upgraded as part
of that run. This caused the upgrade to fail with &quot;unknown command
show-groups&quot; because the old pgd-cli binary does not support that
subcommand. Both tasks are now skipped unless pgdcli or all components are
included in the upgrade.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Updated default AWS AMIs to currently-available images.</summary><hr/><p>The default AWS AMIs that <code>tpaexec configure</code> selects for officially
supported platforms have been updated to currently-available images.
Previously several AMIs (notably Ubuntu 22.04, Ubuntu 24.04, RHEL 8.10
and SLES 15 SP7) had been deregistered by their vendors, causing
<code>tpaexec provision</code> to fail for new clusters on those platforms. RHEL 9
and Rocky 9 also move from minor version 9.5 to 9.7, since 9.5 is no
longer the current minor and updated 9.5 AMIs are not being published.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now derives bdr_version_num automatically from installed BDR/PGD packages.</summary><hr/><p>TPA uses bdr_version_num to make precise decisions about CAMO
configuration. Previously this value was obtained only by querying
the running database, so on an initial deploy (when Postgres is not
yet running) parts of the CAMO configuration could not be rendered
correctly, and users had to set bdr_version_num manually in
config.yml as a workaround. TPA now derives bdr_version_num from
the version of the installed BDR or PGD package on each node, so no
manual setting is required. A value set in config.yml continues to
take precedence, and once the database is running the precise value
reported by bdr.bdr_version_num() remains authoritative.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Stabilised the post-upgrade health check for PGD 6 minor upgrades.</summary><hr/><p>After the last rolling restart of a PGD 6 minor upgrade, BDR
replication slots can briefly remain inactive while they
re-handshake with the just-updated node. The post-upgrade
<code>pgd cluster show --health</code> check could fire during that window
and report a spurious &quot;Replication Slots Critical&quot; failure.
TPA now polls <code>bdr.group_replslots_details</code> for up to two minutes
waiting for all slots to become active before running the strict
health check. If the window is exhausted, the health check runs
anyway so genuine problems are still reported.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue with custom rc-local service creation.</summary><hr/><p>TPA creates a custom systemd service file to ensure the rc-local script is running on startup
on distributions that don't support it out of the box.
This fix changes the location of said service file to comply with conventions and avoid failures
with a missing parent folder that could happen with the previously chosen path.
This fix also ensures that this service file creation task is only applied when it is actually needed.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now normalises Postgres Extended to 'pgextended' &amp; 'edbpge' based on architecture.</summary><hr/><p>A fix has been introduced to normalise Postgres Extended flavours based on architecture.
When choosing any Postgres Extended variant via 'tpaexec configure' (--pgextended, --edbpge, --edb-postgres-extended, or --postgres-flavour pgextended/edbpge),
BDR-Always-ON or older architectures now normalise to 'pgextended', whereas newer architectures normalise to 'edbpge'.
Trying to deploy a 'pgextended' flavour cluster on an incompatible architecture will result in an error, preventing misconfiguration.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue whereby read_listen_port was added to config for BDR versions below 5.5.</summary><hr/><p>tpaexec configure added read_listen_port to default_pgd_proxy_options
even for older BDR versions. A deploy-time assertion was also added to
catch a missing read_listen_port when upgrading to PGD 5.5 or later.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now rejects invalid cluster names at configure time.</summary><hr/><p>Previously, <code>tpaexec configure</code> accepted cluster names that contained
characters such as periods (for example, when the cluster directory
name embedded a version string like <code>v1.1.0-rc.1</code>). The configuration
was written successfully, but a later <code>tpaexec provision</code> then failed
with a fatal assertion because the cluster name did not match the
required pattern <code>^[_a-zA-Z0-9-]+$</code>. <code>tpaexec configure</code> now applies
the same check up front and rejects invalid names before any cluster
directory is created, so the problem is reported immediately rather
than several steps later.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Upgrade playbooks now use systemctl instead of pgrep.</summary><hr/><p>Major and minor PGD upgrade playbooks used <code>pgrep</code> to decide whether
harp-proxy or harp-manager was still running on a node. On hosts where
procps was not installed, two of those checks silently misread &quot;binary
missing&quot; as &quot;process not running&quot;: the BDR 4→5 upgrade then skipped
<code>harpctl unmanage cluster</code> before stopping harp-manager, and the PGD 5
minor upgrade ran the wrong proxy health check.</p>
<p>The checks now use <code>systemctl is-active --quiet</code>, which works on every
distro TPA supports and reflects the authoritative service state.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now omits pgaudit from CIS compliance on EPAS clusters.</summary><hr/><p>Previously, <code>tpaexec configure --compliance cis</code> unconditionally
added <code>pgaudit</code> to <code>extra_postgres_extensions</code>. This caused
startup failures on EPAS, which has built-in audit logging, so
<code>pgaudit</code> is now omitted on EPAS clusters.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now fails deploy when pgbouncer and pgd-proxy share a listen port.</summary><hr/><p>When an instance had both <code>pgbouncer</code> and <code>pgd-proxy</code> in its role list
and both services were configured to listen on the same port (the
default, 6432, on each), only one could bind. pgBouncer typically won
the race and pgd-proxy was left in a failed state, but TPA's deploy
returned success. The misconfiguration only became visible during the
first upgrade, as a <code>FATAL: SSL required</code> error from the
TLS-protection test connecting to pgBouncer instead of pgd-proxy.</p>
<p>TPA now rejects this configuration at deploy time with a clear
message naming the affected host and the two variables involved
(<code>pgbouncer_port</code> and <code>pgd_proxy_options.listen_port</code>). Users who
co-host pgbouncer and pgd-proxy must set one of them to a non-default
value in <code>config.yml</code> so the two listeners don't collide.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now installs pgd-cli on pgd-proxy nodes during the 4to5 upgrade.</summary><hr/><p>The BDR-Always-ON to PGD-Always-ON upgrade adds the pgd-proxy role
to former harp-proxy nodes, but pgd-cli was never installed on
those nodes during the upgrade. The post-upgrade diagnostic then
failed with &quot;No such file or directory&quot;.</p>
<p>The pgdcli-upgrade plays now also target pgd-proxy hosts, so
pgd-cli is in place when the diagnostic runs.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue whereby automatic witness node addition did not work for PGD-X clusters with even data nodes.</summary><hr/><p>When configuring a PGD-X cluster with an even number of
<code>--data-nodes-per-location</code>, <code>tpaexec configure</code> now correctly adds a
witness node to each location. This ensures Raft consensus can be
established without requiring the user to explicitly pass
<code>--add-witness-node-per-location</code>. The behaviour is now consistent with
the PGD-Always-ON architecture and the documented behaviour for PGD-X.
<code>tpaexec configure</code> now also rejects <code>--data-nodes-per-location</code> values
below 2, which previously produced an invalid cluster with no data nodes.</p>
</details></td><td>59694</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now includes the correct mod_wsgi module for PEM server version 10.4.0 and higher.</summary><hr/><p>This change ensures that the correct mod_wsgi module is included for PEM server
versions 10.4.0 and higher, guaranteeing compatibility and proper functionality
for older PEM server versions as well.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now includes additional modules to deploy EPAS in SLES 15.</summary><hr/><p>When deploying a SLES 15 cluster, PackageHub registration is now performed
as part of the initial system registration for both EPAS and PEM installations.
Additionally, two new modules (sle-module-desktop-applications and
sle-module-development-tools) are now enabled for EPAS deployments in order
to install dependencies such as libclang13.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now auto-creates the switch2cm.yml link on older cluster directories.</summary><hr/><p><code>tpaexec switch2cm</code> failed with &quot;the playbook: commands/switch2cm.yml
could not be found&quot; on clusters that were originally configured with
a TPA release predating the <code>switch2cm</code> command, because the cluster
directory had no link to <code>architectures/PGD-Always-ON/commands/switch2cm.yml</code>.</p>
<p><code>tpaexec switch2cm</code> now calls <code>tpaexec relink</code> automatically when the
link is missing, matching the behaviour <code>tpaexec upgrade</code> already had
for its own command link.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue whereby HARP manager symlink creation failed on nodes 2+ during upgrade.</summary><hr/><p>During a rolling upgrade to edbpge, the task that creates a symlink at
/var/run/postgresql/.s.PGSQL.&lt;port&gt; for HARP manager to connect to the
database would fail on all nodes after the first with &quot;refusing to convert
from file to symlink&quot;. The destination path could hold a real socket file
left behind by the pre-upgrade postgres (either from a flavour migration
or from an unclean stop). Adding force: true to the symlink task in all
affected upgrade playbooks ensures the symlink is created correctly
regardless of the prior state of that path.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Introduced retry logic between BDR replication slots when upgrading.</summary><hr/><p>When upgrading the postgresql package version in a BDR cluster, replication slots may take a few seconds to reconnect,
and the upgrade process can have an exit error due to not allocating enough time to the recovery process.
The fix adds retry logic to the health check on the upgrade process to allow time for the slots to recover and avoid
timing-related failures.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now reports a clear error when --location-names doesn't match the architecture's requirement.</summary><hr/><p>Previously, supplying <code>--location-names</code> with the wrong number of names
(for example, a single location for a <code>BDR-Always-ON</code> cluster using the
<code>bronze</code> layout, which requires two) caused <code>tpaexec configure</code> to abort
with an unhandled Python traceback. <code>tpaexec configure</code> now compares the
supplied location names against the number of locations the chosen
architecture and layout actually need, and reports a clear error if they
differ. The change applies to the <code>M1</code>, <code>BDR-Always-ON</code> and
<code>PGD-Always-ON</code> architectures.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue whereby PGD-X demanded too many hostnames in --hostnames-from.</summary><hr/><p>Configuring a PGD-X cluster with --hostnames-from used to fail with
&quot;found only N/16 names matching …&quot; whenever the supplied file held
fewer than 16 names, even when the cluster only needed a handful.
The underlying PGD-X architecture now computes the real number of
instances it will build (data + witness + barman nodes per location,
plus an optional witness-only location and a pemserver if requested),
so --hostnames-from accepts a correctly-sized file and no longer
requires padding it out to 16 entries.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue whereby CAMO configuration was incorrectly added to config.yml.</summary><hr/><p>When configuring a PGD-X cluster with --data-nodes-per-location 2 without
explicitly enabling CAMO via --enable-camo, the generated config.yml would
incorrectly include CAMO commit scopes and partner assignments.
This resulted in unexpected CAMO configuration being applied to clusters
that did not request it.
CAMO configuration is now only generated when --enable-camo is explicitly
passed to tpaexec configure.</p>
</details></td><td>59694</td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue whereby PGD 5/6 minor and 5-to-6 major upgrades occasionally left nodes fenced.</summary><hr/><p>During a node-by-node upgrade, the &quot;Wait for write leader elections to
complete&quot; task could time out because nodes that had been fenced earlier in
the loop were never unfenced: the unfence step did not always see the node's
updated state and was wrongly skipped, leaving the node fenced. TPA now waits
for the node to rejoin Raft consensus and refreshes its state before
unfencing, so nodes are reliably unfenced and the upgrade can proceed.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Improved error output for <code>--primary-location</code> in <code>tpaexec configure</code>.</summary><hr/><p>An error in the argument parsing logic between the <code>--primary-location</code> and the
<code>--location-names</code> arguments when the primary location is not found in the list
of locations caused a built-in Python error to be thrown, which could be
misleading to users, as it is not a TPA-related error.
This fix properly handles the mismatch between the provided
primary location and the list of known locations, avoiding confusing error messages.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed an issue whereby tpaexec configure crashed when --overrides-from was supplied.</summary><hr/><p>Running <code>tpaexec configure</code> with <code>--overrides-from &lt;file&gt;</code> aborted
with &quot;An error was encountered during execution of tpaexec configure:
name 'reduce' is not defined&quot; and no cluster directory was produced.
<code>tpaexec configure --overrides-from</code> now completes successfully and
the values from the supplied YAML file are merged into the generated
cluster configuration as documented.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>switch2cm now works when ansible_user is not root.</summary><hr/><p>Three plays in the PGD-Always-ON switch2cm command did not declare
become at the play level, so the command only worked when
ansible_user was root. With a non-root ansible_user it failed with
&quot;Permission denied&quot; on /pgdata/data/conf.d.</p>
<p>Those plays now explicitly become root, matching the pattern used
elsewhere in PGD-Always-ON. Per-task become overrides for SQL
queries that run as the postgres user are unaffected.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now configures max_active_replication_origins for PostgreSQL 18+ PGD clusters.</summary><hr/><p>TPA now sets the max_active_replication_origins parameter for PGD clusters
on PostgreSQL 18 and above, using &quot;3 * number_of_nodes + 3&quot; — a
safety-margin formula above the &quot;3 origins per peer node&quot; minimum
recommended by the EDB PGD documentation. This prevents PGD node join
failures caused by the default value being too low for multi-node clusters.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TPA now sets path_prefix per backup server in the barman configuration.</summary><hr/><p>Barman's path_prefix was previously set only as a global value in
barman.conf, using the postgres binary directory of the barman host
itself. This caused incorrect behaviour in mixed-version scenarios
(during rolling upgrades, or when the barman host runs a different
PostgreSQL major version than the nodes it backs up).</p>
<p>path_prefix is now also written per backup server in
/etc/barman.d/&lt;backup&gt;.conf, resolving from the backed-up node's
own postgres_bin_dir. This ensures barman uses the correct client
binaries for each node it backs up. The global path_prefix in
barman.conf is unchanged and continues to serve as the default for
same-version scenarios.</p>
<p>When the backed-up node's PostgreSQL version differs from the
barman host's, the matching client packages are now installed on
the barman host automatically so the per-server path_prefix
resolves to real binaries. The barman host must have repository
access for the additional PostgreSQL version (typically the case
with EDB enterprise repositories).</p>
<p>An explicit per-instance override is also available by setting
barman_path_prefix in a node's vars in config.yml.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
</tbody></table>


## Deprecations

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>Removed the deprecated <code>--cohost-proxies</code> &amp; <code>--add-proxy-nodes-per-location</code> configure flags for PGD-X.</summary><hr/><p>The --cohost-proxies &amp; --add-proxy-nodes-per-location flags in PGD-X have now
been removed and will no longer be valid options in <code>tpaexec configure</code> for the PGD-X architecture.
In PGD 6 all proxies are cohosted, making both options obsolete.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
</tbody></table>
